notes: MIL 7-2 final, F5 MIL 6-1. Dominant. Lowder gap=-0.039 regression fully materialized: allowed 6 runs in the F5 window. Sproat was solid. SFG@ARI: V4 F5-rule fired (AWAY/SFG 65.0%) but home SP was TBD — no bet placed per new rule: TBD starters block all signals. Pipeline now enforces this automatically via miss flag.

bet_description: PIT@PHI: (1) F5 HOME/PHI V3 Counter 73.0% (R5) [F5 BET]; (2) FULL AWAY/PIT V4 78.0% (F1+F3+F4) [FULL GAME BET — separate bet]

bet_description: CIN@MIL F5 HOME/MIL — V3 85.0% (R3+R4) [F5 BET ONLY]

bet_description: SFG@ARI FULL AWAY/SFG — V4 F5-rule 65.0% [FULL GAME BET — weak-alignment rule]

Gallen K-BB%=12.83 (just above the R4 threshold of 12) proven as the correct read — he allowed runs. SFG pen (1 tired arm, freshest on slate) held. F5-rule running record: 5W 1L.

bet_description: LAA@SEA F5 AWAY/LAA — V3 84.3% (R1+R6) [F5 BET ONLY]

Ureña (LAA, gap=+0.011) also held SEA scoreless. Both pens completely fresh (0 tired) meant neither starter needed relief. SEA scored in the 6th+ to win 1-0. bet_description: SDP@LAD F5 HOME/LAD — V3 80.0% (R2+R4) [F5 BET ONLY]
notes: LAD 12-7 final, F5 LAD 10-6. Vásquez (gap=-0.034) regression confirmed immediately — LAD scored 10 in the first 5 innings alone. R2 (LAD bat edge wRC+ 119 vs 90) and R4 (Vásquez K-BB%=7.21) both validated decisively. LAD won Sunday Night Baseball wire-to-wire. R2+R4 independent dual confirmation delivered convincingly.

bet_description: CWS@CLE FULL AWAY/CWS — V4 78.0% (F1+F4) [FULL GAME BET]
notes: CLE 4-3 final, F5 CWS 3-1. Reversed window — CWS won the F5 (bat edge showed up early) but CLE came back to win full game 4-3. Kay's weak K-BB%=9.3 eventually caught up in the later innings. If this had been a V3 F5 play, it would have WON (CWS won F5 3-1). V4 full game lost to CLE's late-game surge. bp_cat=BAD for AWAY/CWS (pen quality weaker) materialized late.

bet_description: MIA@ATH F5 HOME/ATH — V3 85.0% (R3+R4) [F5 BET ONLY]
notes: MIA 12-5 final, F5 MIA 7-5. Both windows went AWAY/MIA. Perkins K-BB%=17.63 sweet spot couldn't hold MIA's red-hot offense — 96°F heat + wind blowing out at #2 most offensive park in MLB created conditions that overwhelmed the process signal. MIA was 20-6 in June; Phillips K-BB%=8.28 (weak, R4 target) also held ATH to 5. R3+R4 independent dual-confirmation process signals overridden by park environment + MIA's exceptional offensive form.

bet_description: SDP@LAD F5 HOME/LAD — V3 84.3% (R1+R2) [F5 BET ONLY]
notes: LAD 4-3 final, F5 SDP 2-0. Reversed window — King (SDP) held LAD scoreless through 5 despite gap=-0.027 regression signal. Ohtani allowed 2 to SDP. LAD's 4 runs all came in innings 6-9. Same reversed-window pattern as STL@ATL (7/2) and CWS@CLE today: model direction correct for full game but wrong for the F5 window. R1+R2 are correlated signals — single composite measured twice — vs R3+R4 which are independent. King's K-BB%=15.3 sweet spot and HH%=34.76% meant his regression was moderate, not immediate.

bet_description: STL@CHC F5 HOME/CHC — V3 85.0% (R1+R2+R3+R4+R6) [F5 BET ONLY — five-rule confirmation]
notes: STL 3-0 final, F5 STL 2-0. The strongest signal we have ever generated (five independent rules firing) reversed completely. Leahy's gap=-0.030 regression and HH%=47.7% did NOT materialize into runs allowed — he held CHC scoreless himself while STL's offense broke through against Imanaga (K-BB%=17.8 sweet spot) for 2 in the F5 and a 3rd later. Clean loss on a maximum-confidence play — a reminder that five-rule confirmation still isn't a guarantee, and the model's 85% cap reflects real uncertainty, not near-certainty.

bet_description: MIL@ARI: (1) F5 AWAY/MIL V3 84.3% (R1+R4+R6) [F5 BET]; (2) FULL AWAY/MIL V4 78.0% (F1+F2+F3+F4) [FULL GAME BET — separate bet]
notes: ARI 4-3 final, F5 ARI 2-3 (HOME leaning — wait, home won F5 too). Both legs lost. Woodruff's elite process (K%=28.7%, K-BB%=23.1) didn't prevent MIL from allowing the winning runs late. Full V4 confirmation (all 4 rules) also lost — a rare case of maximum V3+V4 agreement both being wrong.

bet_description: MIL@ARI FULL AWAY/MIL — V4 78.0% (F1+F2+F3+F4) [FULL GAME BET — max V4 confirmation]

Correctly not bet as an F5 play (no V3 signal fired due to composite exceeding R1's window and bp_cat blocking R2) — F5 actually went HOME/ARI (0-1), reinforcing that this was correctly framed as a full-game-only signal.

bet_description: ARI@SDP: (1) F5 AWAY/ARI V3 85.0% (R3+R4) [F5 BET]; (2) FULL AWAY/ARI V4 65.0% (F5-rule) [FULL GAME BET — separate bet]

bet_description: TOR@SFG F5 HOME/SFG — V3 84.3% (R1+R6) [F5 BET ONLY]

Betting against the recent-form narrative paid off decisively.

notes: ARI 9-3 final, F5 ARI 6-2. A blowout loss to a divisional bottom-feeder — the kind of result that raises real questions about Ohtani's outing given the pre-game health notes. Rodriguez's regression signal (which was well-supported by outside sources) did not materialize either — he pitched well.

bet_description: COL@SFG F5 AWAY/COL — V3 Core 82.6% (R3) [F5 BET ONLY — flagged pre-game as the largest model-vs-market conflict in weeks: SFG -156 to -166 favorite, 3 projection models favoring SFG 52-57%, Gordon shelled by this same SFG team in his prior start]
notes: COL 4-3 final (direction correct despite heavy market disagreement), F5 1-1 tie. Gordon's extreme regression signal (gap=+0.0548) partially validated — he held SFG to just 1 through 5 despite his 6.95 ERA and the market heavily favoring SFG. COL's bats came through late for the full-game win. This is a notable result: the model's regression logic outperformed three independent projection systems and the market on direction, even though the F5 window itself only pushed.

bet_description: SEA@TBR F5 AWAY/SEA (counter) — V3 Counter 73.0% (R5) [F5 BET ONLY]
notes: TBR 6-1 final, F5 TBR 2-1. Gilbert's K-matchup edge over TBR's lineup did not translate to a scoreless or low-scoring TBR output — TBR scored early and often. Clean loss on the counter rule, which per our recent backtest (73.3% F5 win rate across 15 decided games) still projects positive but was wrong here.

bet_description: CLE@MIA F5 HOME/MIA — V3 Core 85.0% (R1+R3+R6) [F5 BET ONLY — flagged pre-game as well-supported: numberFire 63.1% MIA, Bibee 3-14-0 ATS]
notes: CLE 4-1 final, F5 CLE 2-0. Despite strong outside confirmation pre-game (numberFire model, Bibee's poor ATS record), Bibee pitched well and CLE's offense got enough against Perez. notes: PHI 4-2 final. Thin signal (none of the three individual edges were compelling on their own pre-game) lost as the lowest-conviction play of the day.

notes: Game postponed 7/17, rescheduled as part of 7/18 doubleheader. This was our strongest signal of the retroactive review (dual V3+V4, 85%/78% confidence) but never got played on its original date. Revisit once today's makeup games are final — do not count this as a decided result.

bet_description: PIT@CLE (G1) F5 AWAY/PIT V3 85.0% (R1+R3) [F5 BET]; FULL AWAY/PIT V4 78.0% (F1+F3+F4) [FULL GAME BET — separate bet]
notes: CORRECTED: repo had conflated PIT@CLE's two doubleheader games into one row, showing Game 2's score (CLE 5, PIT 3) under Game 1's signal data. Verified real Game 1 result via box score: PIT 7-1 final, F5 PIT 2-1. Jones went all 5 innings, 9 K, allowed only Bazzana's RBI triple in the 3rd (Gonzalez's 2-run HR in the 2nd put PIT up 2-0 first). Decisive scoring (Valdez + Gonzales HRs) came in the 6th, after the F5 window. Both legs WIN. This is the THIRD time this exact doubleheader row-conflation bug has appeared (also 7/7, 7/11) — worth prioritizing a pipeline fix for game_num handling in update_scores.py rather than manually catching it each time.
